title: dodonaea viscosa subsp. spatulata
intro: Dodonaea viscosa subsp. spatulata, also known as the stick bush, is a shrubby plant native to Australia, which belongs to the family Sapindaceae. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, due to its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its various health benefits. The health benefits of Dodonaea viscosa subsp. spatulata include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable plant in traditional herbal medicine. In traditional herbal preparations, the leaves of Dodonaea viscosa subsp. spatulata are often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures that can be used to treat various health conditions.
